questions.prompt: You see a patient who has complaints of elbow pain and swelling. The patient works a desk job and spends a lot of time resting their elbow on the armrest of their desk chair. You believe this has caused the swelling seen on the posterior portion of the elbow. What is the most likely diagnosis for this patient?

Your athlete complains of upper right quadrant pain after eating, especially foods like French fries or hotdogs. They have a positive Blumberg's sign. What do you suspect is causing the athlete's symptoms?
A patient has the following signs and symptoms: restlessness, confusion, obtundation, hypotension, tachycardia, respiration changes, and narrowing of pulse pressure. What condition is most likely?
During an evaluation, you noted unilateral scapular winging. Based on this observation, what muscle would you target during treatment?

A runner with chronic Achilles tendinopathy reports morning stiffness and pain during push-off. Symptoms improve with light activity but worsen after activity load increases. Which exercise is most supported by evidence for long-term improvement?
choices: Complete unloading of tendon until symptoms resolve
Progressive loading of the tendon through eccentric exercises

questions.prompt: A swimmer with multidirectional shoulder instability demonstrates poor dynamic control during overhead movements. The clinician designs a program emphasizing neuromuscular control before strengthening. Which progression best reflects appropriate exercise sequencing?
choices: Openchain isotonic strengthening to plyometrics to stabilization
Plyometric throwing drills to resistance training to closedchain control
Closedchain stabilization to openchain control to progressive resistance
Maximal resistance training followed by endurance work

questions.category: Domain 5: Health Administration and Professional Responsibility
questions.prompt: Which of the following budget types requires a clean slate to be the starting point of budget development each year and typically allocates specific budget amounts by categories of expenditures?
Which of the following are typical symptoms of someone suffering from acute stroke?
choices: numbness or weakness of the face, arm, or leg
confusion, speech problems, and cognitive defects

questions.prompt: Which of the following accurately describes fibromyalgia? Select all that apply.
choices: only occurs in patients with depression
responds well to most interventions with known analgesic effects
characterized by widespread muscle, joint, and connective tissue pain
occurs in the absence of tissue injury

questions.category: Domain 1: Risk Reduction, Wellness and Health Literacy
questions.prompt: The athletic trainer needs to determine the possibility of a concussion in a player. When using the Sport Concussion Assessment Tool 6 (SCAT6), in what order should the athletic trainer perform the actions noted to determine the level of the injury? Drag the list items into the correct order.

During the evaluation of a patients knee injury the athletic trainer palpates the tibial tuberosity. On the image below, identify the tibial tuberosity.

Use your mouse and click/drag the circle to the area/region to be identified. You may reposition the circle until you are satisfied with its placement.

questions.title: Focused testlet
questions.scenario: A 70-year-old client presents to your clinic with shoulder pain that is worse at night. The client does not remember any trauma and reports insidious onset. Her AROM is limited in shoulder flexion and abduction. She has a positive painful arc and has weakness in abduction and external rotation.

questions.prompt: 1. What are the most appropriate special tests to perform. Select all that apply.

No change in symptoms is noted with the Spurling’s test, apprehension test, or crank test. The empty can test was positive.

2. Based on the noted shoulder weakness and positive special test, the most likely diagnosis is
3. You would like to start your patient on AAROM exercises for home. What are the most appropriate exercises for your patient to perform at home? Select all that apply.
choices: wall climbs using fingers to assist
bilateral shoulder flexion using a cane
